title: Bipasha Basu

infobox.value: [Full list](/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Bipasha_Basu "List of awards and nominations received by Bipasha Basu")

(Bengali pronunciation: [[bipaʃa boʃu]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Bengali "Help:IPA/Bengali"); born 7 January 1979) is an Indian former actress. Primarily known for her work in [Hindi films](/wiki/Hindi_films "Hindi films"), she [has received](/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Bipasha_Basu "List of awards and nominations received by Bipasha Basu") a [Filmfare Award](/wiki/Filmfare_Awards "Filmfare Awards"). One of the leading faces in the 2000s and early 2010s, she is known for her work in the thriller and horror genres and for several [item numbers](/wiki/Item_number "Item number") and is frequently cited in the media as a [sex symbol](/wiki/Sex_symbol "Sex symbol") and a [scream queen](/wiki/Scream_queen "Scream queen") of her times.

**Bipasha Basu** (Bengali pronunciation: [[bipaʃa boʃu]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Bengali "Help:IPA/Bengali"); born 7 January 1979) is an Indian former actress. Primarily known for her work in [Hindi films](/wiki/Hindi_films "Hindi films"), she [has received](/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Bipasha_Basu "List of awards and nominations received by Bipasha Basu") a [Filmfare Award](/wiki/Filmfare_Awards "Filmfare Awards"). One of the leading faces in the 2000s and early 2010s, she is known for her work in the thriller and horror genres and for several [item numbers](/wiki/Item_number "Item number") and is frequently cited in the media as a [sex symbol](/wiki/Sex_symbol "Sex symbol") and a [scream queen](/wiki/Scream_queen "Scream queen") of her times.

Born in [Delhi](/wiki/Delhi "Delhi") and raised in [Kolkata](/wiki/Kolkata "Kolkata"), Basu pursued a successful career as a fashion model. She then began receiving offers for film roles and made her acting debut with the thriller *[Ajnabee](/wiki/Ajnabee_(2001_film) "Ajnabee (2001 film)")*[[2]](#cite_note-2) (2001), which won her the [Filmfare Award for Best Female Debut](/wiki/Filmfare_Award_for_Best_Female_Debut "Filmfare Award for Best Female Debut"). Basu's first leading role was in the horror film *[Raaz](/wiki/Raaz_(2002_film) "Raaz (2002 film)")*[[3]](#cite_note-3)(2002), for which she earned a nomination for the [Filmfare Award for Best Actress](/wiki/Filmfare_Award_for_Best_Actress "Filmfare Award for Best Actress").[[4]](#cite_note-4) Her career progressed with a leading role in the erotic thriller *[Jism](/wiki/Jism_(2003_film) "Jism (2003 film)")* (2003) and in two consecutive annual top-grossing films, *[No Entry](/wiki/No_Entry "No Entry")* (2005) and *[Dhoom 2](/wiki/Dhoom_2 "Dhoom 2")* (2006).

Basu's other commercially successful films include the drama *[Corporate](/wiki/Corporate_(2006_film) "Corporate (2006 film)")* (2006), the comedies *[Phir Hera Pheri](/wiki/Phir_Hera_Pheri "Phir Hera Pheri")* (2006) and *[All the Best: Fun Begins](/wiki/All_the_Best:_Fun_Begins "All the Best: Fun Begins")* (2009), the thriller *[Race](/wiki/Race_(2008_film) "Race (2008 film)")* (2008), and the romantic comedy *[Bachna Ae Haseeno](/wiki/Bachna_Ae_Haseeno "Bachna Ae Haseeno")* (2008). In the 2010s, she starred in the horror films *[Raaz 3D](/wiki/Raaz_3 "Raaz 3")* (2012), *[Aatma](/wiki/Aatma_(2013_film) "Aatma (2013 film)")* (2013), *[Creature 3D](/wiki/Creature_3D "Creature 3D")* (2014) and *[Alone](/wiki/Alone_(2015_Hindi_film) "Alone (2015 Hindi film)")* (2015). This was followed by a hiatus during which her sole appearance was in the thriller series *[Dangerous](/wiki/Dangerous_(web_series) "Dangerous (web series)")* (2020).

In addition to acting in films, Basu is a fitness enthusiast who has featured in several fitness videos. She hosted the horror series *[Darr Sabko Lagta Hai](/wiki/Darr_Sabko_Lagta_Hai "Darr Sabko Lagta Hai")* in 2015. Following high-profile relationship with actor and model [Dino Morea](/wiki/Dino_Morea "Dino Morea") and [John Abraham](/wiki/John_Abraham "John Abraham"), Basu married actor [Karan Singh Grover](/wiki/Karan_Singh_Grover "Karan Singh Grover") in 2016, with whom she has one daughter. With a networth of 150 Crore INR(USD 16 million), she is one of the richest female celebrities in India.

## Early life

Bipasha Basu was born on 7 January 1979[[5]](#cite_note-Bday-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) to a [Bengali](/wiki/Bengalis "Bengalis") family.[[7]](#cite_note-7) Her father, Hirak, is a [civil engineer](/wiki/Civil_engineer "Civil engineer"), and her mother, Mamta, is a homemaker.[[8]](#cite_note-TOI-Parents-8) She has one elder sister, Bidisha, and one younger sister, Vijayeta.[[8]](#cite_note-TOI-Parents-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9) According to Basu, her name means "deep, dark, desire".[[10]](#cite_note-10)

In Delhi, Basu lived at [Pamposh Enclave](/wiki/Pamposh_Enclave "Pamposh Enclave"), [Nehru Place](/wiki/Nehru_Place "Nehru Place") until age eight and studied at Apeejay High School.[[11]](#cite_note-11) Her family then moved to [Kolkata](/wiki/Kolkata "Kolkata"), where she attended [Bhavan's Gangabux Kanoria Vidyamandir](/wiki/Bhavan%27s_Gangabux_Kanoria_Vidyamandir "Bhavan's Gangabux Kanoria Vidyamandir") in [Bidhannagar](/wiki/Bidhannagar "Bidhannagar").[[12]](#cite_note-12) In her school, Basu was appointed as the [head girl](/wiki/Headgirl "Headgirl") and was fondly called 'Lady [Goonda](/wiki/Goonda "Goonda")' due to her short and commanding personality.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

## Modelling career

In 1996, Basu was spotted at a hotel in Kolkata by the model [Mehr Jesia Rampal](/wiki/Mehr_Jesia_Rampal "Mehr Jesia Rampal"), who suggested she take up modelling.[[14]](#cite_note-Lucky_Bips-14) That year, she participated in and eventually won the Godrej Cinthol Supermodel Contest (organised by [Ford](/wiki/Ford_Motor_Company "Ford Motor Company")), thereby representing India at [Ford Models Supermodel of the World](/wiki/Ford_Models_Supermodel_of_the_World "Ford Models Supermodel of the World") contest in [Miami](/wiki/Miami "Miami").[[15]](#cite_note-The_Rediff_interview-15)[[16]](#cite_note-fmdir-16) Actor [Vinod Khanna](/wiki/Vinod_Khanna "Vinod Khanna"), who was one of the judges in the contest, wanted to cast her alongside his son [Akshaye Khanna](/wiki/Akshaye_Khanna "Akshaye Khanna") in the romance *[Himalay Putra](/wiki/Himalay_Putra "Himalay Putra")* (1997). Basu felt she was too young and declined the role, which eventually went to [Anjala Zaveri](/wiki/Anjala_Zaveri "Anjala Zaveri"). She later appeared in a Calida commercial with her then-boyfriend [Dino Morea](/wiki/Dino_Morea "Dino Morea"), which was controversial for picturing them in a sultry context; she had protesters outside her house as a result.[[15]](#cite_note-The_Rediff_interview-15)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a8/Bipasha_Basu_at_Rocky_S%27s_show_at_Lakme_Fashion_Week_2012_06.jpg/250px-Bipasha_Basu_at_Rocky_S%27s_show_at_Lakme_Fashion_Week_2012_06.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bipasha_Basu_at_Rocky_S%27s_show_at_Lakme_Fashion_Week_2012_06.jpg)

Basu at the [Lakmé Fashion Week](/wiki/Lakm%C3%A9_Fashion_Week "Lakmé Fashion Week") in 2012

With appearances on several magazine covers, Basu continued to pursue a career in fashion modeling during her late teens.[[16]](#cite_note-fmdir-16) At some point, Basu was approached to star opposite [Abhishek Bachchan](/wiki/Abhishek_Bachchan "Abhishek Bachchan") in [J. P. Dutta](/wiki/J._P._Dutta "J. P. Dutta")'s *Aakhari Mughal*, which was supposed to be Bachchan's launch film.[[17]](#cite_note-17) However, the film was cancelled, and Dutta instead made the romantic drama *[Refugee](/wiki/Refugee_(2000_film) "Refugee (2000 film)")* with [Kareena Kapoor](/wiki/Kareena_Kapoor "Kareena Kapoor").[[18]](#cite_note-18) Basu was offered a role opposite [Sunil Shetty](/wiki/Sunil_Shetty "Sunil Shetty") in *Refugee*, which she declined.[[14]](#cite_note-Lucky_Bips-14)

In 1999, Basu featured in an advertisement with [Vivek Oberoi](/wiki/Vivek_Oberoi "Vivek Oberoi"), that was banned from broadcast on Indian television due to scenes that were considered 'bold' at the time, but the ad found its way on social media several years later.[[19]](#cite_note-IE‑ModellingPics2017-19)

Following her transition into acting, Basu continued to appear occasionally in fashion shows and modeling assignments. In 2013, she walked the ramp at the [Lakmé Fashion Week](/wiki/Lakm%C3%A9_Fashion_Week "Lakmé Fashion Week") Summer-Resort for designers Shantanu & Nikhil. Reflecting on the experience, Basu remarked that returning to the runway reminded her of her early modeling days and noted that "modeling is a tough life."[[20]](#cite_note-Modeling2013-20)

## Acting career

See also: [Bipasha Basu filmography](/wiki/Bipasha_Basu_filmography "Bipasha Basu filmography") and [awards and nominations](/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Bipasha_Basu "List of awards and nominations received by Bipasha Basu")

### Debut, breakthrough and rise to prominence (2001–2005)

Basu finally made her acting debut in a negative role opposite [Akshay Kumar](/wiki/Akshay_Kumar "Akshay Kumar") in [Abbas–Mustan](/wiki/Abbas%E2%80%93Mustan "Abbas–Mustan")'s action thriller *[Ajnabee](/wiki/Ajnabee_(2001_film) "Ajnabee (2001 film)")* (2001) which also starred Kapoor and [Bobby Deol](/wiki/Bobby_Deol "Bobby Deol"). The film was inspired by the [American film](/wiki/Cinema_of_the_United_States "Cinema of the United States") *[Consenting Adults](/wiki/Consenting_Adults_(1992_film) "Consenting Adults (1992 film)")* (1992).[[21]](#cite_note-21) *Ajnabee* received mixed reviews from critics upon release, with particular praise for Basu's performance, and it emerged as a moderate commercial success at the box-office.[[22]](#cite_note-22)[[23]](#cite_note-23) Her performance in the film also earned her the [Filmfare Award for Best Female Debut](/wiki/Filmfare_Award_for_Best_Female_Debut "Filmfare Award for Best Female Debut").[[14]](#cite_note-Lucky_Bips-14)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/5c/Dino_Morea_and_Bipasha_Basu.jpg/250px-Dino_Morea_and_Bipasha_Basu.jpg)](/wiki/File:Dino_Morea_and_Bipasha_Basu.jpg)

Basu with her *[Raaz](/wiki/Raaz_(2002_film) "Raaz (2002 film)")* co-star and then partner [Dino Morea](/wiki/Dino_Morea "Dino Morea") in 2002

Basu achieved her breakthrough with [Vikram Bhatt](/wiki/Vikram_Bhatt "Vikram Bhatt")'s supernatural horror thriller *[Raaz](/wiki/Raaz_(2002_film) "Raaz (2002 film)")* (2002), which was the first instalment of the [*Raaz* film series](/wiki/Raaz_(film_series) "Raaz (film series)").[[24]](#cite_note-BO_2002-24) Based on the [Hollywood](/wiki/Cinema_of_the_United_States "Cinema of the United States") film *[What Lies Beneath](/wiki/What_Lies_Beneath "What Lies Beneath")* (2000), it also starred [Dino Morea](/wiki/Dino_Morea "Dino Morea") and debutante [Malini Sharma](/wiki/Malini_Sharma "Malini Sharma"). Portraying a woman in a failing marriage who discovers that her husband is part of a ghostly conspiracy haunting their new home, the film earned Basu widespread critical acclaim and established her in the Hindi film industry. One review in [*The Tribune*](/wiki/The_Tribune_(India) "The Tribune (India)") noted, "...it is Basu who steals the show with her impactful performance."[[25]](#cite_note-25) The film also emerged as a major commercial success at the box office, ranking as the [third highest-grossing Hindi film of the year](/wiki/List_of_Hindi_films_of_2002 "List of Hindi films of 2002").[[26]](#cite_note-26) *Raaz* earned Basu her first nomination for the [Filmfare Award for Best Actress](/wiki/Filmfare_Award_for_Best_Actress "Filmfare Award for Best Actress").[[27]](#cite_note-27) She next starred in [Sanjay Gadhvi](/wiki/Sanjay_Gadhvi "Sanjay Gadhvi")'s romantic comedy *[Mere Yaar Ki Shaadi Hai](/wiki/Mere_Yaar_Ki_Shaadi_Hai "Mere Yaar Ki Shaadi Hai")* (2002) alongside [Uday Chopra](/wiki/Uday_Chopra "Uday Chopra"), [Jimmy Sheirgill](/wiki/Jimmy_Sheirgill "Jimmy Sheirgill") and debutante [Tulip Joshi](/wiki/Tulip_Joshi "Tulip Joshi"). It opened to mixed reviews from critics upon release, with praise for Basu's performance, and emerged as a moderate commercial success at the box office.[[28]](#cite_note-28) Her next film that year, [David Dhawan](/wiki/David_Dhawan "David Dhawan")'s action comedy *[Chor Machaaye Shor](/wiki/Chor_Machaaye_Shor "Chor Machaaye Shor")* alongside Deol, emerged as her first critical and commercial failure.[[29]](#cite_note-29) The same year, she made her [Telugu film](/wiki/Telugu_cinema "Telugu cinema") debut with a supporting role in the [revisionist western](/wiki/Revisionist_Western "Revisionist Western") film *[Takkari Donga](/wiki/Takkari_Donga "Takkari Donga")* opposite [Mahesh Babu](/wiki/Mahesh_Babu "Mahesh Babu") and [Lisa Ray](/wiki/Lisa_Ray "Lisa Ray").[[30]](#cite_note-30) Her next film, the thriller *[Gunaah](/wiki/Gunaah_(2002_film) "Gunaah (2002 film)")*, again opposite Morea, also emerged as a critical and commercial failure.[[24]](#cite_note-BO_2002-24) Her portrayal of a cop who loves a convict and tries to reform him earned her scathing reviews from critics, with Derek Elley of *[Variety](/wiki/Variety_(magazine) "Variety (magazine)")* stating that Basu was miscast as the "idealistic cop".[[31]](#cite_note-31)

In 2003, Basu starred in [Pooja Bhatt](/wiki/Pooja_Bhatt "Pooja Bhatt")'s erotic thriller *[Jism](/wiki/Jism_(2003_film) "Jism (2003 film)")* alongside debutant [John Abraham](/wiki/John_Abraham "John Abraham"), in which she portrayed the seductive, ambitious wife of a millionaire, who indulges in an extramarital affair with an alcoholic lawyer and plots to kill her husband. She and Abraham began a nine year long relationship while shooting for the film. It received mixed reviews upon release, but Basu earned praise for her portrayal of a [femme fatale](/wiki/Femme_fatale "Femme fatale"), with [Taran Adarsh](/wiki/Taran_Adarsh "Taran Adarsh") of *[Bollywood Hungama](/wiki/Bollywood_Hungama "Bollywood Hungama")* commenting, "...the real show stealer is Basu; her sexy look and seductive deep voice, in contrast with her cold and calculating personality, makes her the most impressive [femme fatale](/wiki/Femme_fatale "Femme fatale") since [Zeenat Aman](/wiki/Zeenat_Aman "Zeenat Aman") and [Parveen Babi](/wiki/Parveen_Babi "Parveen Babi")."[[32]](#cite_note-32) The film emerged as a [sleeper hit](/wiki/Sleeper_hit "Sleeper hit") at the box-office, with several trade analysts attributing its commercial success to Basu's popularity.[[33]](#cite_note-Box_Office_2003-33)[[34]](#cite_note-34) *Jism* ranked at 92 in a poll conducted by [Channel 4](/wiki/Channel_4 "Channel 4") the top 100 sexiest movie scenes.[[35]](#cite_note-35) Basu's performance in the film earned her a nomination for the [Filmfare Award for Best Villain](/wiki/Filmfare_Award_for_Best_Performance_in_a_Negative_Role "Filmfare Award for Best Performance in a Negative Role"). However her next release, [Rohit Shetty](/wiki/Rohit_Shetty "Rohit Shetty")'s directorial debut *[Zameen](/wiki/Zameen_(2003_film) "Zameen (2003 film)")* alongside [Ajay Devgn](/wiki/Ajay_Devgn "Ajay Devgn") and [Abhishek Bachchan](/wiki/Abhishek_Bachchan "Abhishek Bachchan"), emerged as a critical and commercial failure.[[33]](#cite_note-Box_Office_2003-33)[[36]](#cite_note-36)

Basu had five releases in 2004, all of which received scathing critical reviews and emerged as commercial disasters at the box office. She began the year with the musical romance *[Ishq Hai Tumse](/wiki/Ishq_Hai_Tumse "Ishq Hai Tumse")* in her third collaboration with Morea. She then collaborated for the second time with [Vikram Bhatt](/wiki/Vikram_Bhatt "Vikram Bhatt") in her next release, the psychological romantic thriller *[Aetbaar](/wiki/Aetbaar "Aetbaar")*. She played the role of a young woman who falls in love with a psychopath. *[Rediff.com](/wiki/Rediff.com "Rediff.com")* noted that "... The characters are not convincing, the plot is not fast-paced or interesting."[[37]](#cite_note-37) Her next film was [Mani Shankar](/wiki/Mani_Shankar "Mani Shankar")'s fantasy science fiction film *[Rudraksh](/wiki/Rudraksh_(film) "Rudraksh (film)")*, based on the Indian epic *[Ramayana](/wiki/Ramayana "Ramayana")*.[[38]](#cite_note-Box_Office_2004-38) She then starred in the supernatural horror film *[Rakht](/wiki/Rakht "Rakht")* as a [tarot card reader](/wiki/Divinatory,_esoteric_and_occult_tarot "Divinatory, esoteric and occult tarot") trying to solve a murder mystery. Shruti Bhasin of *PlanetBollywood* wrote, "Basu impresses in a different look and role."[[39]](#cite_note-39) Her final release that year was [Anil Sharma](/wiki/Anil_Sharma_(director) "Anil Sharma (director)")'s psychological thriller *[Madhoshi](/wiki/Madhoshi "Madhoshi")* opposite Abraham.[[40]](#cite_note-40) Her performance of a mentally unstable woman was generally well received.[[41]](#cite_note-41)

In 2005, she appeared in the thriller *[Chehraa](/wiki/Chehraa "Chehraa")* alongside Morea, which was a critical and commercial failure. This was followed by the musical love triangle *[Barsaat](/wiki/Barsaat_(2005_film) "Barsaat (2005 film)")* alongside Deol and [Priyanka Chopra](/wiki/Priyanka_Chopra "Priyanka Chopra"), which also received mixed-to-negative reviews from critics. Taran Adarsh commented, "As an actor, she [Basu] does show sparks only towards the end."[[42]](#cite_note-42) It also emerged as a commercial failure at the box office.[[43]](#cite_note-43) She next starred in the Tamil romantic comedy *[Sachein](/wiki/Sachein "Sachein")*, alongside [Vijay](/wiki/Vijay_(actor) "Vijay (actor)") and [Genelia D'Souza](/wiki/Genelia_D%27Souza "Genelia D'Souza"), which proved to be a box office hit. She then appeared in [Anees Bazmee](/wiki/Anees_Bazmee "Anees Bazmee")'s comedy *[No Entry](/wiki/No_Entry "No Entry")* which grossed ₹750 million (US$8.9 million) at the box office, ranking as the [highest-grossing Hindi film of the year](/wiki/List_of_Hindi_films_of_2005 "List of Hindi films of 2005").[[44]](#cite_note-44) Basu portrayed a [bargirl](/wiki/Bargirl "Bargirl") who acts as the wife of two men. The film received positive reviews, and she earned her first nomination for the [Filmfare Award for Best Supporting Actress](/wiki/Filmfare_Award_for_Best_Supporting_Actress "Filmfare Award for Best Supporting Actress") for her performance in the film. Her last release of the year was [Prakash Jha](/wiki/Prakash_Jha "Prakash Jha")'s crime action film *[Apaharan](/wiki/Apaharan "Apaharan")*, alongside Devgn and [Nana Patekar](/wiki/Nana_Patekar "Nana Patekar"), which was a critical and commercial success.[[45]](#cite_note-45)[[46]](#cite_note-46) During this period, she refused to act in [art films](/wiki/Parallel_Cinema "Parallel Cinema") due to salary problems.[[47]](#cite_note-47)

### Commercial success and established actress (2006–2009)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/df/Ms_Bipasha_Basu_addressing_a_press_conference_at_Black_Box%2C_Kala_Academy_during_the_37th_International_Film_Festival_%28IFFI-2006%29_in_Panaji%2C_Goa_on_November_25%2C_2006.jpg/250px-thumbnail.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ms_Bipasha_Basu_addressing_a_press_conference_at_Black_Box,_Kala_Academy_during_the_37th_International_Film_Festival_(IFFI-2006)_in_Panaji,_Goa_on_November_25,_2006.jpg)

Basu at a press conference at the [37th International Film Festival of India](/wiki/37th_International_Film_Festival_of_India "37th International Film Festival of India") in 2006

Basu established herself as a leading actress of Hindi cinema in 2006.[[48]](#cite_note-48) The year saw her star in four major releases — the comedy *[Phir Hera Pheri](/wiki/Phir_Hera_Pheri "Phir Hera Pheri")*, the comedy drama *[Corporate](/wiki/Corporate_(2006_film) "Corporate (2006 film)")*, the [crime drama](/wiki/Crime_drama "Crime drama") *[Omkara](/wiki/Omkara_(2006_film) "Omkara (2006 film)")* and the action thriller *[Dhoom 2](/wiki/Dhoom_2 "Dhoom 2")* — all of which performed well critically and commercially.[[49]](#cite_note-Top_Grossers-49)[[50]](#cite_note-50)

*Phir Hera Pheri* saw her star alongside [Akshay Kumar](/wiki/Akshay_Kumar "Akshay Kumar") to portray a con woman who steals ₹10 million from the protagonists. The film received mixed-to-positive reviews and emerged as a commercial success, ranking as the [eighth highest-grossing Hindi film of the year](/wiki/List_of_Hindi_films_of_2006 "List of Hindi films of 2006").[[51]](#cite_note-51) In [Madhur Bhandarkar](/wiki/Madhur_Bhandarkar "Madhur Bhandarkar")'s *Corporate*, she gave up her glamorous look to portray a businesswoman who has no qualms in taking advantage of her sexuality to con the CEO of a rival company. Her performance in the film earned her critical acclaim, as well as her second nomination for the Filmfare Award for Best Actress.[[52]](#cite_note-52) However, it was only an average grosser at the box-office.[[53]](#cite_note-53)

In [Vishal Bhardwaj](/wiki/Vishal_Bhardwaj "Vishal Bhardwaj")'s adaptation of *[Othello](/wiki/Othello "Othello")*, *[Omkara](/wiki/Omkara_(2006_film) "Omkara (2006 film)")*, Basu played the role of Billo Chamanbahar ([Bianca](/wiki/Bianca_(Othello) "Bianca (Othello)")), alongside an [ensemble cast](/wiki/Ensemble_cast "Ensemble cast") of Devgn, [Kareena Kapoor](/wiki/Kareena_Kapoor_Khan "Kareena Kapoor Khan"), [Saif Ali Khan](/wiki/Saif_Ali_Khan "Saif Ali Khan"), [Konkona Sen Sharma](/wiki/Konkona_Sen_Sharma "Konkona Sen Sharma") and [Vivek Oberoi](/wiki/Vivek_Oberoi "Vivek Oberoi"). She caught the attention of the public with her dance number "Beedi", which was highly popular in India and overseas.[[54]](#cite_note-54) Moreover, her performance also earned positive reviews, with critic [Rajeev Masand](/wiki/Rajeev_Masand "Rajeev Masand") of *[CNN-IBN](/wiki/CNN-News18 "CNN-News18")* writing, "...Basu is brought in to lend oomph and she highly succeeds in doing that."[[55]](#cite_note-55)

Basu's last year of 2006 was *[Dhoom 2](/wiki/Dhoom_2 "Dhoom 2")*, where she again starred alongside an ensemble cast of [Hrithik Roshan](/wiki/Hrithik_Roshan "Hrithik Roshan"), Bachchan, [Aishwarya Rai](/wiki/Aishwarya_Rai_Bachchan "Aishwarya Rai Bachchan") and [Uday Chopra](/wiki/Uday_Chopra "Uday Chopra"). She played the [dual role](/wiki/Dual_role "Dual role") of a cop and an [NRI](/wiki/Indian_diaspora "Indian diaspora"). She became the talk of the town for donning a bikini in the film; to prepare for this scene she reportedly ate only oranges for three days and trained hard.[[56]](#cite_note-56) The film, and Basu's performance, received positive reviews from critics upon release, with [Nikhat Kazmi](/wiki/Nikhat_Kazmi "Nikhat Kazmi") of *[The Times of India](/wiki/The_Times_of_India "The Times of India")* commenting, "Aah Bipasha! Looks great, brings back the bikini to Bollywood, and acts well, thus doing more than just slipping into the shadows like a pretty accessory."[[57]](#cite_note-57) *Dhoom 2* emerged as a major blockbuster at the box office, grossing ₹1,513.89 million (US$18 million) worldwide, ranking as the highest-grossing Hindi film of the year and the [highest-grossing Hindi film of all time](/wiki/List_of_highest-grossing_Hindi_films "List of highest-grossing Hindi films") at the time.[[58]](#cite_note-58) As of 2024, it ranks as the highest grosser of Basu's career.[[59]](#cite_note-59)

In 2007, her only appearance was in [Vivek Agnihotri](/wiki/Vivek_Agnihotri "Vivek Agnihotri")'s sports drama *[Dhan Dhana Dhan Goal](/wiki/Goal_(2007_Hindi_film) "Goal (2007 Hindi film)")*. The film saw her perform alongside frequent co-star John Abraham as the physiotherapist of a football team. Despite hype prior to release, it opened to mixed-to-negative reviews from critics, and was a commercial failure at the box office.[[60]](#cite_note-60) Her dance number "Billo Rani" became quite popular and earned her the nickname "Billo Rani", though equal credit is due to *Omkara* in which her name was Billo Chamanbahar.[[61]](#cite_note-61)

In 2008, she teamed up again with Abbas–Mustan for the crime thriller *[Race](/wiki/Race_(2008_film) "Race (2008 film)")*. She played the role of Sonia, who is stuck in a love triangle between two brothers (played by Saif Ali Khan and [Akshaye Khanna](/wiki/Akshaye_Khanna "Akshaye Khanna")) and who ends up murdering one of them. The film was a commercial success at the box office, grossing ₹680 million (US$8.0 million) worldwide, ranking as the [fifth highest-grossing Hindi film of the year](/wiki/List_of_Hindi_films_of_2008 "List of Hindi films of 2008").[[62]](#cite_note-62) Her performance, like the film, was appreciated by critics with Taran Adarsh citing it as "her best work so far. She's superb!"[[63]](#cite_note-63)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/23/Bippashabasuu.jpg/250px-Bippashabasuu.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bippashabasuu.jpg)

Basu at the success party of *[Race](/wiki/Race_(2008_film) "Race (2008 film)")* in 2008

Basu next starred alongside [Ranbir Kapoor](/wiki/Ranbir_Kapoor "Ranbir Kapoor"), [Deepika Padukone](/wiki/Deepika_Padukone "Deepika Padukone") and [Minissha Lamba](/wiki/Minissha_Lamba "Minissha Lamba") in [Siddharth Anand](/wiki/Siddharth_Anand "Siddharth Anand")'s romantic comedy *[Bachna Ae Haseeno](/wiki/Bachna_Ae_Haseeno "Bachna Ae Haseeno")* as a successful supermodel who finds it hard to forgive her ex-lover for betraying her. The film received mixed-to-positive reviews from critics upon release, with widespread praise for Basu's performance. It emerged as a commercial success at the box office, ranking as the [eighth highest-grossing Hindi film of the year](/wiki/List_of_Hindi_films_of_2008 "List of Hindi films of 2008").[[64]](#cite_note-64) Her performance in the film earned her a second nomination for the Filmfare Award for Best Supporting Actress. She completed 2008 with a special appearance in the song "[Phir Milenge Chalte Chalte](/wiki/Phir_Milenge_Chalte_Chalte "Phir Milenge Chalte Chalte")" opposite [Shah Rukh Khan](/wiki/Shah_Rukh_Khan "Shah Rukh Khan") in [Aditya Chopra](/wiki/Aditya_Chopra "Aditya Chopra")'s romantic comedy *[Rab Ne Bana Di Jodi](/wiki/Rab_Ne_Bana_Di_Jodi "Rab Ne Bana Di Jodi")*.[[65]](#cite_note-Item_Number-65)

Her first release in 2009, the science fiction thriller *[Aa Dekhen Zara](/wiki/Aa_Dekhen_Zara "Aa Dekhen Zara")* opposite [Neil Nitin Mukesh](/wiki/Neil_Nitin_Mukesh "Neil Nitin Mukesh"), received mixed reviews from critics upon release, and emerged as a commercial failure at the box office.[[66]](#cite_note-66) However, her next film, Rohit Shetty's comedy *[All the Best: Fun Begins](/wiki/All_the_Best:_Fun_Begins "All the Best: Fun Begins")* performed moderately well, with her comic role being appreciated by fans.[[67]](#cite_note-67)[[68]](#cite_note-68)

Basu later appeared in an unglamorous and challenging role in [Rituparno Ghosh](/wiki/Rituparno_Ghosh "Rituparno Ghosh")'s Bengali film, *[Shob Charitro Kalponik](/wiki/Shob_Charitro_Kalponik "Shob Charitro Kalponik")*. "I love saris and I'm wearing a lot of them in Ritu's film," she said.[[69]](#cite_note-69) The film, and her performance, received high praise from critics upon release. Critic Subhash K. Jha spoke high of her, and said, "Basu pulls out all stops to deliver her career best performance. Her moments of anguish before and after her screen-husband's death are expressed in tones of cathartic conviction that we never knew existed within Basu." He also compared her with the multiple-award-winning actress, [Shabana Azmi](/wiki/Shabana_Azmi "Shabana Azmi"), commenting that Basu's anguished portrayal of bereavement could only be equalled by Azmi.[[70]](#cite_note-70)

### Continued work (2010–2013)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/74/Cast_of_pankh.jpg/330px-Cast_of_pankh.jpg)](/wiki/File:Cast_of_pankh.jpg)

Basu with the cast of *[Pankh](/wiki/Pankh "Pankh")* (2010)

Basu's first release in 2010 was Sudipto Chattopadhyaya's *[Pankh](/wiki/Pankh "Pankh")*, where she apperared as an imaginary character who exists in the protagonist's mind.[[71]](#cite_note-71) She was then featured as a [Kashmiri](/wiki/Kashmiri_people "Kashmiri people") girl alongside [Sanjay Dutt](/wiki/Sanjay_Dutt "Sanjay Dutt"), [Anupam Kher](/wiki/Anupam_Kher "Anupam Kher") and [Kunal Kapoor](/wiki/Kunal_Kapoor_(actor,_born_1977) "Kunal Kapoor (actor, born 1977)") in [Rahul Dholakia](/wiki/Rahul_Dholakia "Rahul Dholakia")'s drama thriller *[Lamhaa](/wiki/Lamhaa "Lamhaa")*, which explored socio-political problems in Kashmir.[[72]](#cite_note-72) Principal photography of *Lamhaa* was disrupted many times by the locals due to its controversial theme.[[73]](#cite_note-73) During the shooting of *Lamhaa*, she left the sets at [Anantnag](/wiki/Anantnag "Anantnag") and headed back to [Mumbai](/wiki/Mumbai "Mumbai"), citing she was afraid of the crowd there. Producer Bunty Walia considered replacing her with [Vidya Balan](/wiki/Vidya_Balan "Vidya Balan"), but she returned for the shoot later with necessary security arrangements.[[74]](#cite_note-74)[[75]](#cite_note-75) *Lamhaa* opened to mixed reviews from critics upon release, but Basu's performance received praise. *NDTV Movies* wrote, "Basu sinks herself into her character imparting a dramatic resonance into the role without resorting to stock expressions. The sequence where she gets mauled by militant women is as traumatic to watch as it must have been for Basu to shoot."[[76]](#cite_note-76)

Basu's next appearance that year was in [Priyadarshan](/wiki/Priyadarshan "Priyadarshan")'s action thriller *[Aakrosh](/wiki/Aakrosh_(2010_film) "Aakrosh (2010 film)")* as the wife of a ruthless cop who helps the police investigating honor killings in their village. [Nikhat Kazmi](/wiki/Nikhat_Kazmi "Nikhat Kazmi") of *[The Times of India](/wiki/The_Times_of_India "The Times of India")* commented, "Basu actually doesn't have much to do and does seem to be miscast as the much-abused, bruised and battered wife of [Paresh Rawal](/wiki/Paresh_Rawal "Paresh Rawal")".[[77]](#cite_note-77) Both *Lamhaa* and *Aakrosh* were commercial failures at the box office.[[78]](#cite_note-78)[[79]](#cite_note-79)

Basu's next release was the 2011 action thriller *[Dum Maaro Dum](/wiki/Dum_Maaro_Dum_(film) "Dum Maaro Dum (film)")*, alongside [Abhishek Bachchan](/wiki/Abhishek_Bachchan "Abhishek Bachchan") and [Rana Daggubati](/wiki/Rana_Daggubati "Rana Daggubati"). Talking of the film, she said, "My next release is *Dum Maaro Dum*, in which I play a lively, colourful, happy-go-lucky girl called Zoey, who is the representation of Goa as we see it."[[80]](#cite_note-80) The film performed reasonably well at the box office and met with mixed-to-positive reviews from critics.[[81]](#cite_note-81)[[82]](#cite_note-82)

Basu then finished shooting for her first English film project, [Roland Joffé](/wiki/Roland_Joff%C3%A9 "Roland Joffé")'s Belgian [romance](/wiki/Romance_film "Romance film") [time travel](/wiki/Time_travel_in_fiction "Time travel in fiction") [adventure](/wiki/Adventure_film "Adventure film") *[Singularity](/wiki/The_Lovers_(2013_film) "The Lovers (2013 film)")* in which she portrayed a [Maratha](/wiki/Maratha "Maratha") warrior.[[83]](#cite_note-83) The film then went through several delays and release struggles for nearly two years.

In 2012, Basu collaborated with [Abbas–Mustan](/wiki/Abbas%E2%80%93Mustan "Abbas–Mustan") for the third time in the heist action thriller *[Players](/wiki/Players_(2012_film) "Players (2012 film)")*, which became the first Hindi film release of 2012.[[84]](#cite_note-84) The film was an official remake of *[The Italian Job](/wiki/The_Italian_Job_(2003_film) "The Italian Job (2003 film)")*.[[85]](#cite_note-85) Majorly hyped prior to release, *Players* was a commercial disaster at the box office, and was negatively compared to *The Italian Job* and highly criticised for its execution and performances, including that of Basu.[[86]](#cite_note-86)[[87]](#cite_note-87)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4f/Bipasha_Basu_promotes_%27Raaz_3D%27_%282%29.jpg/250px-Bipasha_Basu_promotes_%27Raaz_3D%27_%282%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bipasha_Basu_promotes_%27Raaz_3D%27_(2).jpg)

Basu promoting *[Raaz 3](/wiki/Raaz_3 "Raaz 3")* in 2012

Basu's next release of the year was the supernatural horror thriller *[Raaz 3: The Third Dimension](/wiki/Raaz_3:_The_Third_Dimension "Raaz 3: The Third Dimension")* directed by [Vikram Bhatt](/wiki/Vikram_Bhatt "Vikram Bhatt"). Co-starring [Emraan Hashmi](/wiki/Emraan_Hashmi "Emraan Hashmi") and [Esha Gupta](/wiki/Esha_Gupta "Esha Gupta"), the film proved a major commercial success at the box office, grossing ₹1.01 billion (US$12 million) worldwide. It emerged as the highest-grossing Hindi horror film of all time and became Basu's first commercial success after a period of relatively poor reception. The film opened to mixed-to-positive reviews upon release, with high praise for Basu's performance. Subhash K. Jha of IANS said, "It's Basu who holds together the feverish proceedings. She delivers a full-bodied gutsy performance."[[88]](#cite_note-88)[[89]](#cite_note-89)

In 2013, Basu first appeared in a guest appearance in *[Race 2](/wiki/Race_2 "Race 2")*, a direct sequel of *Race* (2008), where she reprised her role of Sonia Martin from the first film, in a flashback scene.[[90]](#cite_note-90)[[91]](#cite_note-91) She then starred alongside [Nawazuddin Siddiqui](/wiki/Nawazuddin_Siddiqui "Nawazuddin Siddiqui") in the horror film [*Aatma*](/wiki/Aatma_(2013_film) "Aatma (2013 film)") directed by Suparn Verma. The film and Basu's performance received mixed reviews from critics upon release. [Filmfare](/wiki/Filmfare "Filmfare") stated, "This is one of Bipasha's better performances. She emotes well even in the really tough scenes where she is being abused."[[92]](#cite_note-92)[[93]](#cite_note-93) Another reviewer from [India TV](/wiki/India_TV "India TV") news noted that "Bipasha Basu as a frightening mother is decent" and remarked that her performance in *Raaz 3D* was stronger.[[94]](#cite_note-94) [NDTV](/wiki/NDTV "NDTV") praised Basu's deep role, but also commented that they felt the film wasn't particularly frightening.[[95]](#cite_note-95)

The same year, Basu's English film *Singularity*, now renamed as *[The Lovers](/wiki/The_Lovers_(2013_film) "The Lovers (2013 film)")* finally released, but she received mixed performance for her performance in that film as well.[[96]](#cite_note-96) The film remains Basu's only foreign film to date.

### Setbacks, hiatus and occasional work (2014–2020)

In 2014, Basu appeared in two films of diametrically opposite genres – the first was [Sajid Khan](/wiki/Sajid_Khan_(director) "Sajid Khan (director)")'s comedy *[Humshakals](/wiki/Humshakals "Humshakals")*. The film was a critical and commercial disaster, and is considered [one of the worst Bollywood films ever made](/wiki/List_of_films_considered_the_worst#Humshakals_(2014) "List of films considered the worst"). Basu didn't even participate in the film's promotions because she was "extremely disturbed by the result" and stated, "*Humshakals* was the worst experience of my life".[[97]](#cite_note-97)[[98]](#cite_note-98)[[99]](#cite_note-99)

Basu's second release was [Vikram Bhatt](/wiki/Vikram_Bhatt "Vikram Bhatt")'s monster horror film *[Creature 3D](/wiki/Creature_3D "Creature 3D")* alongside debutant [Imran Abbas](/wiki/Imran_Abbas "Imran Abbas"). This film was another critical and commercial failure.[[100]](#cite_note-100) Regarding Basu's role, [Anupama Chopra](/wiki/Anupama_Chopra "Anupama Chopra") of [Film Companion](/wiki/Film_Companion "Film Companion") wrote, "Basu plays Ahana, the owner of a boutique hotel while her admirer is played by the totally forgettable actor (Abbas). The creature had more personality than all of them put together."[[101]](#cite_note-101)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/83/Bipasha_Basu_unveils_Health%E2%80%99s_latest_magazine_cover_%283%29.jpg/250px-Bipasha_Basu_unveils_Health%E2%80%99s_latest_magazine_cover_%283%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bipasha_Basu_unveils_Health%E2%80%99s_latest_magazine_cover_(3).jpg)

Basu at an event in 2017

In 2015, Basu featured in [Bhushan Patel](/wiki/Bhushan_Patel "Bhushan Patel")'s romance horror film [*Alone*](/wiki/Alone_(2015_Hindi_film) "Alone (2015 Hindi film)") opposite [Karan Singh Grover](/wiki/Karan_Singh_Grover "Karan Singh Grover").[[102]](#cite_note-102) The film performed moderately well at the box office, and received mixed reviews from critics upon release, with praise for Basu's [dual role](/wiki/Dual_role "Dual role").[[103]](#cite_note-103) *[India TV](/wiki/India_TV "India TV")* reviewed the film and wrote that, "With some genuine scary moments and Basu-Grover's scorching chemistry, *Alone* stands apart from numerous horrible horror flicks made in the past," and also stated, "Basu just takes her act to another level."[[104]](#cite_note-104)

Basu also made her debut on Hindi [television](/wiki/Indian_television_drama "Indian television drama") in 2015, by hosting [&TV](/wiki/%26TV "&TV")'s anthology horror fiction series *[Darr Sabko Lagta Hai](/wiki/Darr_Sabko_Lagta_Hai "Darr Sabko Lagta Hai")*.

Following her marriage to her *Alone* co-star [Karan Singh Grover](/wiki/Karan_Singh_Grover "Karan Singh Grover") in 2016, Basu retired from acting to focus on her personal life. Two years later, she made a cameo appearance as herself in the comedy [*Welcome to New York*](/wiki/Welcome_to_New_York_(2018_film) "Welcome to New York (2018 film)") (2018).

In 2020, she made a brief return with her [OTT](/wiki/Over-the-top_media_service "Over-the-top media service") debut in the [MX Player](/wiki/MX_Player "MX Player")'s crime thriller series *[Dangerous](/wiki/Dangerous_(web_series) "Dangerous (web series)")*, also starring her husband Grover. The web series received mixed-to-negative reviews from critics upon release.[[105]](#cite_note-105) A critic from *Hindustan Times* observed that Basu, as Neha Singh, attempts to "raise the heat" alongside Karan Singh Grover, but her performance is undermined by rushed plot twists and an underwritten character.[[106]](#cite_note-106) *[News18.com](/wiki/News18_India "News18 India")* gave the series a rating of 1 star, saying that, "A bland and clichéd plot topped with forced sexual tension makes *Dangerous* a rather tedious watch."[[107]](#cite_note-107)

## Other ventures

### Music video appearances

In 1998, Basu appeared in the music video "Tu" for [Sonu Nigam](/wiki/Sonu_Nigam "Sonu Nigam")'s album, *[Kismat](/wiki/Kismat_(1998_Album) "Kismat (1998 Album)")* that got her noticed by many Bollywood stalwarts and helped her in getting film offers.[[108]](#cite_note-Basu's_Video_Apperance-108) In 2000, Basu was again seen in Sonu Nigam's album *Jaan*, in the song "Tera Milna".[[19]](#cite_note-IE‑ModellingPics2017-19) In 2004, she made a guest appearance in [Jay Sean](/wiki/Jay_Sean "Jay Sean")'s music video "[Stolen](/wiki/Stolen_(Jay_Sean_song) "Stolen (Jay Sean song)")".[[108]](#cite_note-Basu's_Video_Apperance-108)

### Fitness and wellness projects

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/bc/Bipasha_appointed_as_new_brand_ambassador_of_Pantene_01.jpg/250px-Bipasha_appointed_as_new_brand_ambassador_of_Pantene_01.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bipasha_appointed_as_new_brand_ambassador_of_Pantene_01.jpg)

Basu at the [Pantene](/wiki/Pantene "Pantene") launch in 2012

Aside from films, Basu has been an advocate of physical fitness. In 2005, she released her first fitness DVD jointly with [John Abraham](/wiki/John_Abraham_(actor) "John Abraham (actor)"), titled *Bollywood Bodies*.[[109]](#cite_note-109)

In 2010 she released her first solo fitness DVD called *Love Yourself: Fit & Fabulous You*, which emphasised being strong, healthy, and loving oneself.[[110]](#cite_note-110) The DVD consisted of a 60-day routine for weight loss, including a beginner's workout, an advanced workout and an "easy tone" workout. She was also the brand ambassador of Sugar Free, which helped her in promoting the DVD.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Hiren Gada, director of [Shemaroo Entertainment](/wiki/Shemaroo_Entertainment "Shemaroo Entertainment"), said, "A celebrity like Basu, genuinely believes in staying healthy. She is also passionate about fitness."[[111]](#cite_note-111)

In September 2011, Basu launched her second fitness DVD titled *Break Free*, a 30-minute dance routine, and the follow-up to Basu's first workout video, continuing the *Love Yourself* series.[[112]](#cite_note-breakfree-112)[[113]](#cite_note-113)

In January 2014, Basu launched the third instalment of her *Love Yourself* DVD fitness series, *Unleash*, an advanced training routine including plyometrics and focusing more on power and speed, while strengthening the bones and improving muscle coordination.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Later in 2014, many of her fitness workout videos were released for free on distributor Shemaroo Entertainment's health-focussed [YouTube](/wiki/YouTube "YouTube") channel, Shemaroo Good Health 24/7.

### Stage performances

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/5f/Bipasha_Basu_at_the_%27IIFA_2015_Fashion_Extravaganza%27.jpg/250px-Bipasha_Basu_at_the_%27IIFA_2015_Fashion_Extravaganza%27.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bipasha_Basu_at_the_%27IIFA_2015_Fashion_Extravaganza%27.jpg)

Basu at the [IIFA Fashion Extravaganza](/wiki/16th_IIFA_Awards "16th IIFA Awards") in 2015

Basu has been a regular performer at several Hindi film award ceremonies like Filmfare and IIFA.[[114]](#cite_note-IIFAExuberance-114) In the summer of 2013, she performed live alongside [Atif Aslam](/wiki/Atif_Aslam "Atif Aslam"), [Shaan](/wiki/Shaan_(singer) "Shaan (singer)") and [Malaika Arora](/wiki/Malaika_Arora "Malaika Arora") for the first time in a series of concerts in [Birmingham](/wiki/Birmingham "Birmingham") and [London](/wiki/London "London").[[115]](#cite_note-115)[[116]](#cite_note-116) In July 2020, Basu posted on her Instagram account, expressing how she misses the energy and exuberance of performing in front of a live audience and adding that she "can't wait for it to happen again soon."[[114]](#cite_note-IIFAExuberance-114)

### Lifestyle brand

In 2015, Basu, [Malaika Arora](/wiki/Malaika_Arora "Malaika Arora"), and [Sussanne Khan](/wiki/Sussanne_Khan "Sussanne Khan") launched their own shopping portal "The Label Life". Basu's section of the portal included makeup, statement jewels, shoes and bags.[[117]](#cite_note-117)[[118]](#cite_note-118)

## In the media

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0e/Bipasha-basu-filmfare.JPG/250px-Bipasha-basu-filmfare.JPG)](/wiki/File:Bipasha-basu-filmfare.JPG)

Basu at the launch of [Filmfare](/wiki/Filmfare "Filmfare") cover issue in 2009

Basu is considered as one of the most popular actresses of 2000s in [Hindi cinema](/wiki/Hindi_cinema "Hindi cinema") and frequently cited in the media as a [sex symbol](/wiki/Sex_symbol "Sex symbol").[[119]](#cite_note-119) She is also known by a variety of nicknames that play on her given name, including Bonnie, Bips, Bip, B, BB, and Bona.[[120]](#cite_note-120)

*[Filmfare](/wiki/Filmfare "Filmfare")* placed her 7th and 9th in their "Top Ten Actresses" list of 2003 and 2004 respectively.[[121]](#cite_note-121) *[Rediff.com](/wiki/Rediff.com "Rediff.com")* placed her 2nd in its "Bollywood's Most Beautiful Actresses" list in 2004.[[122]](#cite_note-122) In *Rediff.com*'s "Best Bollywood Actresses", Basu was placed 3rd in 2005 and 7th in 2006 and 1st in 2008[[123]](#cite_note-123)[[124]](#cite_note-124)

In 2006, she became the Most Desirable Woman in India.[[125]](#cite_note-125)[[126]](#cite_note-126)[[127]](#cite_note-127) *[Eastern Eye](/wiki/Eastern_Eye_(newspaper) "Eastern Eye (newspaper)")* named her the "Sexiest Woman in Asia" in 2005 and 2007. In 2009, she topped the *[Maxim](/wiki/Maxim_(India) "Maxim (India)")*'s "Hot 100" list.[[128]](#cite_note-128)

On the [*Times* 50 Most Desirable Women](/wiki/The_Times_of_India "The Times of India") list, she ranked 8th in 2011, 13th in 2012, and 7th in 2013. She appeared in *[Forbes India](/wiki/Forbes_India "Forbes India")*'s 2012 [Celebrity 100](/wiki/Forbes_Celebrity_100 "Forbes Celebrity 100") list, where she placed 25th with an estimated annual income of ₹268.5 million (US$3.2 million).[[129]](#cite_note-129).She also appeared from 2013-2016 in Forbes Celebrity 100 list with the lowest ranking 71st.[[130]](#cite_note-130) She is the only Indian actress to be featured in the World Fashion Model Directory in Global Top 1500 whereas her fellow colleague Ujjwala Raut is in Top 5000. [[131]](#cite_note-131)

In 2013, she was ranked at 67 as one of the World's Most 99 Desirable Women by AskMen.com. *[Playboy](/wiki/Playboy "Playboy")* magazine also ranked her at 7 as one of the top 10 desirable women in the world.[[132]](#cite_note-132)[[133]](#cite_note-Poll-133)

Basu is also a celebrity endorser for brands including [Dabur](/wiki/Dabur "Dabur"), [Pantene](/wiki/Pantene "Pantene"), [Emami](/wiki/Emami "Emami"), and [Alkermes](/wiki/Alkermes_(company) "Alkermes (company)").[[134]](#cite_note-134)[[135]](#cite_note-135)

## Personal life

Basu met [Dino Morea](/wiki/Dino_Morea "Dino Morea") on a blind date arranged by their friends in 1998, while Morea was a popular supermodel at that time.[[136]](#cite_note-136) They were a celebrated and talked-about pair and featured in multiple films and modeling assignments.[[137]](#cite_note-137) The couple ended their relationship in 2002.[[138]](#cite_note-138) Years later, Morea admitted that he initiated the end of their relationship during the filming of *[Raaz](/wiki/Raaz_(2002_film) "Raaz (2002 film)")* (2002), and noted that it was tough on Basu and she was upset.[[139]](#cite_note-139)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d9/Karan_Singh_Grover_and_Bipasha_Basu_at_the_Reception.jpg/250px-Karan_Singh_Grover_and_Bipasha_Basu_at_the_Reception.jpg)](/wiki/File:Karan_Singh_Grover_and_Bipasha_Basu_at_the_Reception.jpg)

Basu with husband [Karan Singh Grover](/wiki/Karan_Singh_Grover "Karan Singh Grover"), at their Reception in 2016

Post her break-up with Morea, Basu was reported to be dating her *Jism* co-star [John Abraham](/wiki/John_Abraham "John Abraham").[[140]](#cite_note-Bipasha_and_John_Relationship-140) Both Basu and Abraham maintained a prominent relationship for nine years, from 2003 to their eventual breakup in 2011.[[141]](#cite_note-141)[[140]](#cite_note-Bipasha_and_John_Relationship-140) Basu then briefly dated actor [Harman Baweja](/wiki/Harman_Baweja "Harman Baweja"), but they broke up in 2014.[[142]](#cite_note-142)[[143]](#cite_note-143)

While shooting for *[Alone](/wiki/Alone_(2015_Hindi_film) "Alone (2015 Hindi film)")* in 2014, Basu began dating her co-star [Karan Singh Grover](/wiki/Karan_Singh_Grover "Karan Singh Grover"), a popular television actor known for his work in Hindi TV series like *[Dill Mill Gayye](/wiki/Dill_Mill_Gayye "Dill Mill Gayye")* and *[Qubool Hai](/wiki/Qubool_Hai "Qubool Hai")*.[[144]](#cite_note-WedDate-144)

Basu and Grover eventually tied the knot on 30 April 2016 in a traditional [Bengali wedding](/wiki/Bengali_Hindu_wedding "Bengali Hindu wedding") ceremony, after dating for over a year.[[144]](#cite_note-WedDate-144) While the wedding was an intimate affair, their wedding reception was attended by many prominent celebrities from film and television industry, such as [Dino Morea](/wiki/Dino_Morea "Dino Morea"), [Amitabh Bacchan](/wiki/Amitabh_Bacchan "Amitabh Bacchan"), [Salman Khan](/wiki/Salman_Khan "Salman Khan"), [Shah Rukh Khan](/wiki/Shah_Rukh_Khan "Shah Rukh Khan"), [Aishwarya Rai](/wiki/Aishwarya_Rai "Aishwarya Rai"), [Tabu](/wiki/Tabu_(actress) "Tabu (actress)"), [Ranbir Kapoor](/wiki/Ranbir_Kapoor "Ranbir Kapoor"), [Sunil Grover](/wiki/Sunil_Grover "Sunil Grover"), [Karan Kundra](/wiki/Karan_Kundra "Karan Kundra"), and [Drashti Dhami](/wiki/Drashti_Dhami "Drashti Dhami"), among many others.[[145]](#cite_note-145)[[146]](#cite_note-146)

Basu and Grover welcomed their first child, a baby girl named Devi Basu Singh Grover in November 2022.[[147]](#cite_note-147) In 2023, Basu revealed that Devi was diagnosed with [ventricular septal defect](/wiki/Ventricular_septal_defect "Ventricular septal defect") (VSD), a condition involving two holes in the heart, just three days after birth.[[148]](#cite_note-148) Devi underwent open‑heart surgery when she was three months old, and has been doing well since the successful procedure.[[149]](#cite_note-149)
